Pythagorean Theorem Calculator

Find the missing side of a right triangle. Enter any two sides to calculate the third using a² + b² = c².

FAQ

What is the Pythagorean theorem?

In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse (c) equals the sum of squares of the other two sides: a² + b² = c².

How to find the hypotenuse?

c = √(a² + b²). Enter the two legs (a and b) and calculate the square root of their squares' sum.
